# SushiSwap v3
> SushiSwap v3 is Sushi’s multichain concentrated-liquidity automated market maker; this independent dashboard provides information without custodying assets or executing trades.

## What is SushiSwap v3
SushiSwap v3 is a decentralized exchange protocol in which smart-contract pools exchange token pairs without an order book or centralized custodian. Liquidity providers choose price ranges where their capital participates in swaps and earns pool fees. This concentrated-liquidity design uses capital more efficiently than SushiSwap v2’s default full-range liquidity, but requires more active management. It follows the Uniswap v3 architecture while using Sushi’s own deployments, interface, and routing infrastructure.

## How it works
1. A trader selects a network, input token, output token, amount, slippage limit, and route; an ERC-20 approval may be required first.
2. The wallet signs a transaction, and routing contracts execute the quoted path through one or more liquidity pools.
3. Pool contracts calculate output from available liquidity, deduct the pool fee, enforce the minimum output, and settle the swap atomically on-chain.
4. Liquidity providers deposit two assets, select a fee tier and price range, and receive an NFT representing the position; fees accrue only while its liquidity is active for trades.

## Key facts
- SushiSwap v3 is non-custodial, permissionless smart-contract infrastructure rather than a centralized exchange.
- Pools are distinguished by blockchain, token pair, and fee tier.
- Ethereum Mainnet uses chain ID 1; Sushi also publishes deployments for numerous other EVM networks.
- Ethereum’s verified SushiSwap v3 factory is `0xbACEB8eC6b9355Dfc0269C18bac9d6E2Bdc29C4F`.
- Pool assets generally use the ERC-20 interface; native assets are represented through wrapped tokens in contracts.
- Liquidity positions use an ERC-721-compatible NFT containing the pool, range, liquidity, and fee-accounting state.
- The SUSHI token is not required to make an ordinary swap or create a position.
- Transactions settle according to the selected network’s block inclusion and finality rules.

## Common questions
**Is SushiSwap v3 safe?** It removes centralized custody, but smart-contract bugs, malicious tokens, phishing, adverse prices, and wallet-approval risks remain.

**Who controls my assets?** You control your wallet keys. During liquidity provision, deposited assets reside in pool contracts until the position owner removes them.

**What fees do users pay?** Traders pay the selected pool’s fee tier plus network gas; liquidity providers receive pool fees attributable to their active liquidity.

**How long does a transaction take?** A swap appears after its transaction confirms; timing depends on the network and congestion. A first-time token approval requires an additional transaction.

**Which assets and chains are supported?** Availability depends on deployed contracts, existing pools, and the current Sushi interface; unlisted tokens are not automatically vetted.

**How do I start?** Use the official Sushi interface, connect a compatible wallet, select the correct network and tokens, review approvals and minimum output, then confirm in the wallet.

**What are the main LP risks?** Price divergence, impermanent loss, out-of-range inactivity, one-sided asset exposure, contract risk, and gas costs can outweigh earned fees.

## App
- Dashboard: https://sushiswap-v3.github.io/
- App: https://sushiswap-v3.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
Official Sushi app: https://www.sushi.com/ethereum/swap
Official pool interface: https://www.sushi.com/ethereum/pool
Sushi v3 AMM FAQ: https://www.sushi.com/faq/product/pool/what-is-v3-amm
Sushi v3 liquidity guide: https://www.sushi.com/academy/how-to-v3
Sushi v3 launch overview: https://www.sushi.com/blog/sushi-concentrated-liquidity-v3-is-live
V3 core source code: https://github.com/sushiswap/v3-core
V3 periphery source code: https://github.com/sushiswap/v3-periphery

Core audit archive: https://github.com/sushiswap/v3-core/tree/master/audits
Official X: https://x.com/SushiSwap

## Data & references
Ethereum v3 factory on Etherscan: https://etherscan.io/address/0xbACEB8eC6b9355Dfc0269C18bac9d6E2Bdc29C4F
SushiSwap v3 on DefiLlama: https://defillama.com/protocol/sushiswap-v3
SushiSwap v3 Ethereum markets on CoinGecko: https://www.coingecko.com/en/exchanges/sushiswap-v3-ethereum
Ethereum Mainnet chain data: https://chainlist.org/chain/1
ERC-20 standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-20
ERC-721 standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-721
